microsoft_ft: **Scaled Azure's recovery validation program** from a 2-person operation into a platformized system, standardizing workflows to sustain 45+ annual resilience drills and drive **$14M+ business impact** across enterprise customers including Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp.

Led **0→1 development** of an internal Resilience Automation Platform, defining product requirements and self-service scheduling capabilities that reduced operational toil by 30% and transitioned execution to a scalable, repeatable model.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive resilience testing capability, delivering a rack-level drill program in 4 months with a **94% recovery rate**, surfacing critical hardware defects and establishing a new continuous validation standard.

Directed **14 cross-org recovery executions** under executive visibility, including serving as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a $1.5B+ enterprise contract, aligning engineering, ops, and partner stakeholders.

Built an internal **AI agent for drill planning**, restructuring workflows to cut planning cycle time by 39% and increase drill capacity by 21%, demonstrating product-led automation applied to high-stakes infrastructure operations.
microsoft_2023: Championed **product adoption** for AI-driven code generation workflows, running user demos and training sessions that drove utilization across 14 key Azure teams and saved **37 engineering hours monthly**.

Shaped the product roadmap to include intent-based YAML generation by conducting **11+ user interviews** with Azure service teams, translating qualitative feedback into prioritized feature requirements with clear user value.

Improved data integrity and discoverability by migrating documentation to an **AI-powered semantic search** tool with rigorous metadata standards, cutting lookup time by 83% and improving cross-team knowledge access.
microsoft_2022: Generated **$3M in accelerated revenue** and launched regions 28% faster by securing cross-functional alignment on a unified automation prioritization framework spanning 140+ engineering and operations teams.

Defined a product strategy to reduce launch toil by conducting discovery with 20+ service teams, identifying **81 hours of manual effort** per region and building a roadmap to automate the highest-impact critical paths.

Built a **Power BI dashboard** tracking operational toil across 140+ teams, enabling leadership to surface automation gaps and direct roadmap investments toward the highest-value, highest-friction workflows.
amazon_robotics: Achieved **zero operational downtime** during a 2,000+ unit pilot transition by defining the legacy OS migration strategy and mapping dependencies across 1,200+ stations in a complex, multi-team robotics environment.

Facilitated Agile ceremonies including sprint planning and retrospectives, prioritizing the backlog to resolve **40+ high-priority tickets** and stabilize the developer intake process across cross-functional engineering teams.

Drove alignment between IT, Operations, and Engineering to implement **automated CI/CD pipelines**, accelerating the software deployment cycle by 25% and reducing manual release overhead across robotics fulfillment systems.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by optimizing end-to-end scoping and invoicing workflows for 200+ monthly proposals, introducing a structured CRM process that improved conversion consistency and reduced turnaround time.

Reduced **Customer Acquisition Cost by 13%** and boosted conversions by 2.7% by executing a digital-first go-to-market strategy, optimizing the website and Google Business profile to improve funnel performance.

Improved leadership pricing models by conducting **financial profitability analysis** across 1,000+ project records, identifying and closing margin leaks that had been obscured across high-volume, variable-cost service jobs.

tailoring_notes: Trimmed to 5 bullets for microsoft_ft and 3 each for all other roles to resolve page overflow while staying within min/max caps.
Mirrored Meta JD vocabulary throughout: cross-functional teams, product requirements, roadmap, user research, adoption, automation, and data-informed decision-making.
Bold emphasis targets metrics and outcome nouns recruiters scan first, kept to 1–2 spans per bullet per directive.
AI/automation bullets (AI agent, semantic search, CI/CD, LLM tools) are preserved and surfaced prominently to match Meta's central-products platform and infra orientation.
